reviews.review_title: Easily the best hand tracking game
reviews.review_description:
First off I love how the game controls, SBR feels like you're in the battle field and not just playing a video game anymore.  I love how you can fire as many projectiles as fast as possible by just pushing the air rapidly, you just feel overpowered. The boss fight is also really fun too, even though it goes down super fast if you spam the air-push attack.

My biggest complaint is I'm not sure if this game is even finished.  The game does a great job at telling me how to play the game and all, but there's only 5 levels and they all end super fast.  Even the boss goes down within a matter of seconds.  

Is there more gameplay beyond the tutorial? Because I have gotten the mission complete message at the end of all 5 levels and there's nothing left.  I'm not sure if this game is still in early access or if I need to unlock an arcade mode of some sort.

This is a good game otherwise and I hope it continues to receive more content.

The controls in this game is amazing. Target aim with my gaze, making VR fighting so much more enjoyable. You have to experience it to appreciate. Here are some rare and unused discounts to speed up your decision.

The learning curve of vr is like boom this is cool… then you keep playing and stumble upon games like this and actually realize this is the type of intuitive gameplay you envisioned having access to as a child and it’s finally come to reality… thanks so much for creating this game I looked at it and I bought it hesitating can’t wait to see what the future holds for this release 🕊️🔥🔥🔥
It features excellent movement and experience, fully utilizing the hand recognition capabilities. It's a game that Quest players must purchase, and it's definitely worth it.

25% off referral code at the bottom in my YouTube link

I really like the game and how good the handtracking feels.....this is definitely the best movement system for handtracking but definitely needs some work and make it a little less sensitive or at least have sensitivity options in the menu.... i love the movement and how you can dodge stuff with your intense speed like your in a anime like dbz, personally I do boxing dodge movements to dodge projectiles and it makes me feel so cool doing it.

 the game is a bit much for the price and it being short but still alot of fun...I hope you guys expand on the game and add more playable characters and custom hands like different gloves and cybernetic hands....would be cool to have different elements like air and electricity, would be sick to throw lightnings bolts and strikes or air pushing and tornadoes..

if you guys need a beta tester for the game to help improve on it i would love to, I have tested for a few different vr games over the last few years

while I dont 100% agree with the price (now in this early state) either way I would still recommend getting it beacuse of how cool and unique it feels and its the only fast paced handtracking anime fighting game in existence at the moment 

https://youtu.be/NAZTrb97ay8

I really hope your working on this game still, it looks very amazing, I was literally thinking VR needed a game like this and a stumbled upon it. I would love for this to be finished one day 

….only complaint is the left hand is facing backwards but honestly I’m willing to wait

developer_response.body:
Please be aware that SBR is supposed to play in hand-tracking mode. There's an issue that we can't mark it as hand-tracking-only on AppLab, and we're already working on it. 

We encourage you to give it another try in hand-tracking mode. The tutorial had been translated into English now, and we're excited to hear about your thought on the gesture-based control!
